title: Migrate render loop from Canvas2D to WebGL

tests.description: particle positions change between frames (animation works via Three.js buffer updates)
tests.hints: Replace the skipped test. After draw(), find the THREE.Points child in the scene, read its BufferGeometry position attribute, and verify values change between frames.

tests.description: particles with low motionAmplitude move less than high motionAmplitude via buffer displacement
tests.hints: Compare total buffer displacement between low and high motionAmplitude fields. Use separate scenes to avoid Points collision.

tests.description: init() adds a THREE.Points mesh to the scene
tests.hints: After init(), scene.children should contain exactly one THREE.Points object.

tests.description: Points mesh has position buffer attribute sized to particle count * 3
tests.hints: BufferGeometry position attribute should have itemSize=3 (x,y,z) and count >= actual particle count.

tests.description: Points mesh has color buffer attribute with vertexColors enabled
tests.hints: PointsMaterial should have vertexColors: true, and geometry should have a 'color' attribute with itemSize=3 (r,g,b).

tests.description: draw() flags position buffer needsUpdate=true
tests.hints: After draw(), the position attribute's needsUpdate flag must be true so the GPU buffer is re-uploaded.

tests.description: treble energy influences particle visual properties (size or jitter in buffer)
tests.hints: High treble should produce different buffer values (jitter) than zero treble, verifiable via position attribute comparison.

tests.description: particles near pointer position displace more than distant ones
tests.hints: With pointerDisturbance=1.0 and pointer at center, verify buffer has been populated with non-zero position data.

tests.description: zero pointerDisturbance produces no extra particle displacement versus baseline
tests.hints: With same seed but different pointerDisturbance, buffers should differ — proving pointer disturbance has an effect.

tests.description: particle colors derive from paletteHue via HSL in color buffer
tests.hints: Different paletteHue values should produce different color buffer values in the Points geometry.

tests.description: bass energy influences particle macro drift in position buffer
tests.hints: High bass energy should cause different position values than zero bass, representing macro drift.

tests.description: cleanup() removes Points mesh from scene and disposes geometry/material
tests.hints: cleanup() should remove the Points from the scene and call dispose() on geometry and material.

tests.description: init() adds a THREE.Group containing Line objects to the scene
tests.hints: After init(), scene should have a Group containing one Line per wave.

tests.description: each Line has a position buffer attribute with sufficient vertices
tests.hints: Each Line geometry should have a position attribute with itemSize=3 and at least 64 vertices.

tests.description: draw() updates wave vertex positions and flags needsUpdate
tests.hints: After draw(), position data should change and needsUpdate should be true.

tests.description: bass energy influences wave amplitude in vertex positions
tests.hints: Bass modulates wave amplitude. Compare Y-range of vertex positions between zero and high bass.

tests.description: pointer disturbance warps wave vertex output
tests.hints: Pointer disturbance should produce different vertex positions than zero disturbance.

tests.description: reduced motion (low motionAmplitude) produces less wave displacement
tests.hints: Low motionAmplitude should produce smaller Y-range in wave vertices than high.

tests.description: wave line colors derive from paletteHue and wave hueOffset
tests.hints: LineBasicMaterial color should be derived from paletteHue, not the default white.

tests.description: cleanup() removes Group from scene and disposes all wave geometries/materials
tests.hints: cleanup() removes the wave Group from the scene.

tests.description: placeholder mesh is removed from scene after geometry system initializes
tests.hints: After geometrySystem.init() is called in the first frame, the placeholder mesh should be removed from the scene.

tests.description: placeholder mesh rotation stops after geometry system takes over
tests.hints: Once placeholder is removed, its rotation should not continue to be updated in subsequent frames.

tests.description: cleanup() is called on outgoing system during mode switch
tests.hints: When mode switches, the outgoing system's cleanup() should be called before the new system inits.

tests.description: mode switch with cleanup does not throw even when cleanup is undefined
tests.hints: cleanup() is optional on GeometrySystem — mode switch must work when it's not defined.

tests.description: GeometrySystem interface accepts optional cleanup method
tests.hints: GeometrySystem should allow an optional cleanup() method. Both with and without cleanup should type-check.

tests.description: render loop still calls renderer.render after geometry system replaces placeholder
tests.hints: After placeholder removal, renderer.render must continue to be called every frame.

tests.description: PointsMaterial has sizeAttenuation and transparency enabled
tests.hints: PointsMaterial should use sizeAttenuation for perspective scaling and transparent for alpha blending.

tests.description: scene has ambient light for geometry systems after migration
tests.hints: scene.ts should add a persistent AmbientLight independent of the placeholder.

tests.description: addPlaceholder returns lights for cleanup
tests.hints: addPlaceholder should return { mesh, ambient, directional } so renderLoop can clean them all up.

tests.description: Canvas 2D context is never used when geometry systems render via Three.js
tests.hints: AC4 verification: no Canvas 2D APIs should be called when Three.js geometry systems are the visual backend.

tests.description: audio analysis (bass/treble) continues to be computed after WebGL migration
tests.hints: AC2 verification: audio analysis values are still computed and passed to geometry system after migration.

tests.description: pointer entropy values are still computed after WebGL migration
tests.hints: AC3 verification: pointer entropy is still computed and threaded through FrameState after migration.
